title: Egg and chips
subtitle: Oven chips with fried eggs and a crisp, lacey edge
excerpt: Egg and chips is one of the great British quick meals. Oven chips do the heavy work in the oven while a couple of eggs are fried in butter on the hob. The yolk breaks into the chips at the table and that is the whole point.
sourceNotes: Standard British midweek meal, common in cafes, chip shops, and home kitchens. The combination of fried egg and chips is one of the most eaten meals in Britain and has been a cafe staple since at least the early 20th century.

content.text: Egg and chips is a meal where the chips do most of the work in the oven while you are free to do other things. The egg goes on just before the chips are ready. The goal is a crisp, well-coloured chip alongside a fried egg with a just-runny yolk and a slightly crisp white edge. Butter gives a better fried egg than oil.
Heat the oven to 220°C fan. Spread the frozen chips in a single layer on a baking tray. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, turning once halfway through, until golden and crisp.

When the chips have about 5 minutes left, melt the butter in a frying pan over a medium heat until it starts to foam. Crack in the eggs and fry for 2 to 3 minutes until the whites are set and the edges are lightly crisp. The yolk should still be runny.
Serve the chips alongside the eggs. Season with salt and pepper. Ketchup on the side is not optional for many people.

items.symptom: The chips are pale and soft
items.cause: Overcrowded baking tray or temperature too low.
items.fix: Use two trays if needed so the chips are in a single layer. They need space around them to crisp rather than steam.

items.symptom: The egg white is set but the yolk has gone hard
items.cause: Too high a heat or too long in the pan.
items.fix: Fry over a medium heat and keep a close eye on it. Two to three minutes is usually enough.

items.symptom: The egg sticks to the pan
items.cause: Not enough butter, or the pan was not hot enough before the egg went in.
items.fix: Let the butter foam before adding the egg, and use a non-stick pan.

items.symptom: The chips and eggs are not ready at the same time
items.cause: The egg was started too early or too late.
items.fix: Start the egg when the chips have about 5 minutes left. They come together quickly.

content.text: Egg and chips has been a British cafe staple since the early 20th century and became a weekly dinner institution in working households. It is one of the cheapest meals to make, takes under half an hour, and satisfies in a way that more elaborate food often does not. The version cooked at home is generally better than the cafe version because the butter in the egg is not rationed.
